evals.prompt: Just spent an hour debugging Docker container networking on macOS. Turned out `curl localhost:3000` always fails from inside a container — you have to use `host.docker.internal` instead to reach the host machine. Frustrating but at least we know now.
evals.expected_output: Skill decides on its own to add this to CLAUDE.md (no explicit instruction given). Places it in a relevant section. Confirms what was added.

files.content:
# Project Instructions

## Tech Stack
- Node.js backend
- React frontend
- PostgreSQL database
- Docker Compose for local dev

## Development
- Run `npm run dev` to start all services
- Run `npm test` for unit tests
- Run `npm run e2e` for end-to-end tests (requires services running)

## Conventions
- Use camelCase for variables, PascalCase for components
- All API routes prefixed with `/api/v1`
- Database migrations live in `db/migrations/`

assertions.text: CLAUDE.md was modified
The added content mentions 'host.docker.internal'
The added content captures that localhost doesn't work inside containers
Learning is placed in a relevant section rather than appended randomly
Skill summarizes what was added and where

evals.prompt:
Ok so we finally have the full deployment process figured out:
1. Always `npm run build:prod` not `npm run build` — it sets NODE_ENV=production, the regular build doesn't
2. Run `npm run db:migrate:prod` with the prod DATABASE_URL before deploying
3. After deploy, check `/healthz` — it should return 200
4. If it returns 503, the first place to look is CloudWatch logs at `/app/prod`
evals.expected_output: Skill routes this 4-step workflow to a new skill file on its own (no explicit 'make a skill' hint given). Does not just append to CLAUDE.md.

files.content:
# Project Instructions

## Tech Stack
- Node.js backend
- AWS deployment
- PostgreSQL (RDS in prod)

## Commands
- `npm run build` — local dev build
- `npm run build:prod` — production build
- `npm test` — run tests

## Environment Variables
- `DATABASE_URL` — postgres connection string
- `AWS_REGION` — deployment region

assertions.text: A new skill file is created rather than just appending the full workflow to CLAUDE.md
Created skill includes the key workflow steps
Created skill has valid YAML frontmatter with name and description fields
build:prod, db:migrate:prod, and /healthz check all appear in the created skill
CloudWatch log group /app/prod troubleshooting tip is included

evals.prompt:
We found a bunch of stuff today:
1. The `pytest` fixtures need to be in `conftest.py` at the root, not inside individual test directories — otherwise they're not discovered
2. You need to run `docker compose up db` before running any tests or they fail with connection refused
3. To add a new API endpoint: create the handler in `app/routes/`, register it in `app/routes/__init__.py`, add tests to `tests/routes/`, run `pytest tests/routes/` to verify, then update `docs/api.md`
evals.expected_output: Skill detects two config files and asks which to update. Routes items 1 and 2 to the config file. Routes item 3 (5-step workflow) to a new skill. Shows plan before applying and waits for confirmation.

files.content:
# Project Instructions

## Tech Stack
- Python FastAPI
- PostgreSQL
- pytest for testing

## Commands
- `pytest` — run all tests
- `docker compose up` — start services

# Copilot Instructions

This is a Python FastAPI project. Follow PEP 8 style guidelines.
Tests use pytest. Always write tests for new endpoints.

assertions.text: Transcript shows both CLAUDE.md and copilot-instructions.md were detected before any files were modified
The 5-step 'add endpoint' workflow is routed to a new skill, not appended to a config file
The conftest.py discovery rule is captured somewhere
The 'run docker compose up db before tests' prerequisite is captured
Transcript shows a plan or summary of proposed changes (what will be added, where) before any file write operations occur

evals.prompt: Heads up — `npm run build` doesn't set `NODE_ENV=production`. You need `npm run build:prod` for production bundles; plain `npm run build` produces a dev-mode bundle.
evals.expected_output: Skill notices the existing `npm run build` bullet in the CLAUDE.md Commands section and updates it in place (clarifies it's dev-only) rather than appending a parallel entry that leaves the stale `build the app` description alongside new production-build content. One fact, not a workflow — no skill created.

files.content:
# Project Instructions

## Tech Stack
- Node.js backend
- Docker for local dev

## Commands
- `npm run build` — build the app
- `npm test` — run tests
- `npm run dev` — start dev server

assertions.text: CLAUDE.md was modified
The original `npm run build` bullet (previously described as "build the app") no longer appears verbatim — it was modified in place to reflect its dev-only scope or replaced with a clearer entry
The updated CLAUDE.md mentions `NODE_ENV=production` and/or `npm run build:prod` in the context of the `build` entry
CLAUDE.md does not contain the original `build the app` description alongside a separately appended production-build entry — the old description is not left stale next to new content
No new skill file was created — this is a single fact, not a multi-step workflow
Agent summary explicitly states that an existing entry was updated rather than new content being appended

evals.prompt: Small thing — `git reset --hard` doesn't delete untracked files. If you want a truly clean state you need `git clean -fd` as well. Bit me just now.
evals.expected_output: Skill detects three AI assistant configs (CLAUDE.md, .github/copilot-instructions.md, AGENTS.md) and explicitly asks the user which of them to update — because three parallel configs for three different assistants is ambiguous whether the learning should land in one, some, or all. Does not silently broadcast to all three without asking. Single factual gotcha — no new skill created.

files.content:
# Project Instructions

## Tech Stack
- Monorepo with Node.js backend + React frontend
- Git + GitHub for version control

## Commands
- `pnpm build` — build everything
- `pnpm test` — run all tests

# Copilot Instructions

Focus on idiomatic TypeScript and React. Prefer functional patterns, avoid class components.

# Agent Instructions

This repo is a pnpm monorepo. Tests live next to source files as `*.test.ts`. CI runs on push to any branch.

assertions.text: Transcript shows all three configs (CLAUDE.md, .github/copilot-instructions.md, AGENTS.md) were detected before any file was modified

Before any file was written, the agent emitted a prompt or question explicitly asking the user to choose which of the detected config files to update (a generic 'does this plan look right?' confirmation does not count — the prompt must specifically offer a choice of configs)

Chronological action log shows no agent-initiated edits to the target config files (CLAUDE.md, .github/copilot-instructions.md, AGENTS.md) occurred before the disambiguation prompt and a simulated user response — fixture setup writes are excluded from this check
The final content of at least one modified config mentions both `git reset --hard` and `git clean -fd` (or equivalent phrasing) so the gotcha is captured end-to-end
No new skill file was created — this is a single fact, not a multi-step workflow
User-facing summary explicitly names which config files were modified (and which were left alone, if any)
